# Recommender System — Leakage Audit

**Date:** 2026-04-29  
**Dataset:** Online Retail II (UCI, UK transactions 2009–2011)  
**Auditor:** auto-generated by `train_recommender.py`

---

## Audit Checklist

### 1. Train/Test Split

| Check | Status | Detail |
|-------|--------|--------|
| Test items held out before any model fits | **PASS** | `leave_one_out_split()` zeroes each user's last purchase *before* returning `train_matrix`. Models only receive `train_matrix`. |
| Test item selection uses temporal ordering | **PASS** | Last interaction selected by `InvoiceDate` sort — not row order or random. |
| Users with only one interaction excluded from test | **PASS** | Condition `matrix.getrow(u_i).nnz < 2` prevents users from having zero training items. |

### 2. Feature Construction

| Check | Status | Detail |
|-------|--------|--------|
| Popularity baseline uses train only | **PASS** | `PopularityRecommender.fit(train_matrix)` — zero from the full matrix before calling `fit`. |
| SVD factorises train only | **PASS** | `SVDRecommender.fit(train_matrix)`. The held-out cell is zero in `train_matrix`. |
| ALS fits on train only (BM25 weighted) | **PASS** | `ALSRecommender.fit(train_matrix)`. BM25 weighting also applied to `train_matrix`. |
| Content-based uses item descriptions only | **PASS** | TF-IDF + NMF run on item description text — no user-item signal. Not a leakage vector. |
| Hybrid combines SVD + CB scores at query time | **PASS** | Both sub-models trained on train only. Score combination is a read-only operation. |

### 3. Evaluation Protocol

| Check | Status | Detail |
|-------|--------|--------|
| Evaluation function receives held-out items | **PASS** | `evaluate_all()` takes `test_items` dict produced by `leave_one_out_split()`. |
| Seen items excluded from recommendations | **PASS** | Each model masks `train_matrix.getrow(u).indices` with `-inf` before `argsort`. |
| Popularity baseline included | **PASS** | Always evaluated first. If sophisticated models do not beat it by >0.5pp Recall@10, the implementation is flagged as suspect. |

### 4. Potential Leakage Vectors — Declared Safe

| Vector | Assessment |
|--------|-----------|
| Item metadata (Description, Price) | Safe — item descriptions are static catalogue attributes, not derived from purchase behaviour at time t. |
| NMF topic vectors from descriptions | Safe — NMF applied to text, not to user-item data. |
| BM25 weighting | Safe — normalises the *training* matrix only. No future information encoded. |
| SVD explained variance ratio | Safe — derived from training singular values, not from test outcomes. |

### 5. Known Structural Limitation

**Leave-one-out is a proxy, not a business metric.** Predicting the *last* item exactly is a difficult task; Recall@10 of 0.02–0.10 is typical for e-commerce. The relative ranking (Hybrid > SVD > Popularity) is the meaningful signal, not the absolute numbers.

If any sophisticated model achieves Recall@10 ≥ 0.50, this is a red flag for leakage — report immediately.

---

## Analogy to Forecasting Leakage Audit

The pattern here mirrors the two leakage bugs discovered in `src/forecasting/features.py`:

| Forecasting | Recommender |
|-------------|-------------|
| `retail_real` used `retail_sales[t]` (target at t) | Contemporaneous NMF topics from LDA used sub-industry sales[t] (additive components of target) |
| Fix: shift feature by 1 period | Fix: use description text (orthogonal to purchase behaviour) |
| PCA reverse-validation confirmed the leak | Popularity baseline acts as sanity check — a model with access to future data would beat it overwhelmingly |

In both scenarios: **if the model knows the answer, it will get a suspiciously low error.**
